description: Baku, the capital of Azerbaijan, is a vibrant city where modernity meets rich history. Situated on the Caspian Sea, Baku is known for its stunning skyline, with futuristic architecture such as the Flame Towers and the Heydar Aliyev Center, designed by Zaha Hadid. The city is also home to a wealth of historical landmarks, including the UNESCO-listed Old City (Icherisheher), where visitors can explore ancient mosques, the iconic Maiden Tower, and the Shirvanshahs' Palace. Baku offers a blend of cultural experiences, from its bustling bazaars and traditional tea houses to modern shopping malls and world-class restaurants. The city's vibrant nightlife and annual events, such as the Formula 1 Grand Prix and Baku International Jazz Festival, make it a dynamic destination. With its blend of Eastern and Western influences, stunning waterfront views, and a mix of modern and ancient architecture, Baku is a must-visit destination for history, culture, and architecture enthusiasts.

faqs.title: 1.)What is the best time to visit Baku?
faqs.content: The best time to visit Baku is in the spring (April to June) or fall (September to November) when the weather is mild and pleasant. Summers can be quite hot, with temperatures often exceeding 30°C (86°F).

faqs.title: 2.)Do I need a visa to visit Baku?
faqs.content: Visitors from many countries, including the EU, US, and UK, require a visa to enter Azerbaijan. However, an e-visa is available for citizens of several countries, which can be applied for online.

faqs.title: 3.)What are the must-see attractions in Baku?
faqs.content: Must-see attractions include the Flame Towers, the Old City (Icherisheher), the Maiden Tower, the Shirvanshahs' Palace, the Heydar Aliyev Center, the Baku Boulevard along the Caspian Sea, and the modern Flame Towers. Don't miss the Gobustan Rock Art Cultural Landscape, just outside the city.

faqs.title: 4.)What language is spoken in Baku?
faqs.content: The official language is Azerbaijani (Azeri), but Russian and English are widely spoken, especially in tourist areas, hotels, and restaurants.

faqs.title: 5.)What are some cultural etiquette tips for visitors to Baku?
faqs.content: When visiting religious sites, remove your shoes and dress modestly. It’s polite to greet people with a handshake, especially when meeting someone for the first time. Showing respect for local traditions and customs is appreciated, especially when interacting with locals in more traditional areas.
